title: Bounded Query-Adaptive Arbitration over Frozen Teacher-Anchor Packet Bundles
note: Compiled compatibility view for the active query-adaptive paper line. The underlying outline candidate remains outline-002 while this paper branch now narrows the story to a bounded query-adaptive arbitration result on the current widened teacher-anchor retrieval surface.
story: The paper story starts from a validated upstream machine-oriented compression pipeline whose frozen teacher-anchor packet bundle is already exportable but whose downstream retrieval behavior remains sensitive to route choice. The current paper line makes a deliberately narrow claim: keep the packet surface fixed, then test whether query-adaptive arbitration over two existing static routes can recover more retrieval signal on one bounded widened surface. The contribution is therefore a bounded feasibility result about downstream readout over a frozen packet surface, not a new coding architecture or a broadly validated machine-facing adapter.
ten_questions: What exactly is preserved by the frozen teacher-anchor packet bundle on the current widened surface?
Why is downstream route choice the main bottleneck after the packet surface is frozen?
How do the raw-global and temporal-context routes fail differently on this surface?
Can a query-adaptive arbitration rule recover more headline retrieval signal without changing the upstream packets?
Does the adaptive gain indicate real downstream-usable structure in the frozen packet surface?
What limitation prevents this result from being written as a broad machine-facing adapter claim?
What is the smallest fair evidence package for this bounded paper line?
What future evidence would be needed before claiming broader downstream generalization?

detailed_outline.title: Bounded Query-Adaptive Arbitration over Frozen Teacher-Anchor Packet Bundles
detailed_outline.abstract: This paper studies whether query-adaptive arbitration can extract more downstream retrieval signal from a frozen teacher-anchor packet bundle than two existing static routes on one bounded widened surface.
research_questions: Can query-adaptive arbitration improve headline retrieval over both static parent routes on the same bounded widened teacher-anchor surface?
If such a gain appears, what does it imply about the amount of downstream-recoverable structure already present in the frozen packet surface?
detailed_outline.methodology: Hold the upstream packet bundle, evaluation harness, and local retrieval setup fixed; compare raw-global, temporal-context, and query-adaptive arbitration on the same bounded surface.
experimental_designs: One bounded evaluation package comparing the two static parent routes and the query-adaptive rule on the same widened teacher-anchor surface.
contributions: A bounded query-adaptive arbitration result that improves headline retrieval on the fixed widened teacher-anchor surface.
An evidence-backed interpretation that the frozen packet surface contains downstream-recoverable structure that static routing leaves underused on that same surface.

sections.title: Appendix robustness note: wider-surface QB-Norm revalidation
sections.note: Appendix-only robustness evidence that narrows broader route-stability claims: wider-surface QB-Norm regularizes geometry but does not improve top-1 retrieval over the raw route.
